compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Root cause (proven, joe-debug): read-CLI option parsers in _engine_support/reads_cli.py match ONLY '--opt=value' (e.g. _cmd_session_logs matches arg.startswith('--limit='); _cmd_list matches '--status=' etc). Space form falls to else -> 'Error: unknown option', exit 2 (confirmed: 'session-logs --limit 3' exit=2; '--limit=3' exit=0; same for 'list --status open'). Inconsistent with _cmd_ready (accepts both '--epic' and '--epic='), composer/write commands (claim --assignee <you>), and CLAUDE.md docs which use space form throughout. Resolution decision: accept-BOTH in read-CLI (matches ready+composer+docs); equals-only everywhere would break documented write-command space forms (large breaking change). Fix: add space-form handling to read-CLI flags + document convention in --help + regression tests for space and equals forms.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Resolved in PR #93 (commit d755bfe4e). Added _coalesce_value_opts normalizer in reads_cli.py so list/session-logs/search/list-epics/ready accept BOTH '--opt value' and '--opt=value' (matching ready/composer/--output/docs). Malformed values + unknown options still error. Documented accept-both in docs/exit-codes.md. Regression tests: tests/interfaces/facades/test_cli_space_form_options.py (3 tests, RED->GREEN, mutation-checked). Verified: 'session-logs --limit 5' now exit 0; 'list --status open' exit 0; '--limit abc' still rejected.

compiled_state.description:
`rebar session-logs` silently returns nothing when the limit is passed in the
space-separated form (`--limit 30`) instead of the equals form (`--limit=30`).
The read-side CLI commands only accept `--opt=value`, while write/composer
commands (and even `ready` in the same module) accept the `--opt value` space
form, so the option-value syntax is inconsistent across the CLI and the repo's
own docs use the space form (e.g. `claim <id> --assignee <you>`). An agent
following that convention runs `session-logs --limit N`, gets a 2-line error on
stderr, and — if stderr is discarded or the output is piped — sees an empty
result and wrongly concludes there are no session logs.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Ensure session logs exist (`rebar session-logs` with no args lists them).
2. Run `rebar session-logs --limit 5` (space-separated form).
3. Observe: `Error: unknown option '--limit'` + usage on stderr, exit code 2.
4. Run `rebar session-logs --limit=5` (equals form) → works, exit 0.
5. Same split elsewhere: `rebar list --status open` → exit 2; `--status=open` → 0.

## Expected
- `--limit 30` (space form) is accepted, OR option-value syntax is consistent
  across all CLI commands and matches the documented `--opt <value>` convention.
- A flag-parsing error is never mistaken for "no results": the symptom that the
  command "comes back empty" should not happen for a mere arg-form mismatch.

## Actual
- `_cmd_session_logs` (src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py:236-252) matches
  only `arg.startswith("--limit=")`; the space form falls to the `else` →
  `unknown option '--limit'`, exit 2.
- Inconsistency is intra-file: `_cmd_ready` (reads_cli.py:302-311) accepts BOTH
  `--epic <id>` and `--epic=<id>`; `claim --assignee <you>` (composer parser,
  src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:477-480) accepts the space form too.

## Notes
- Root cause is arg-form parsing, not the session-log store: data is present and
  the equals form returns it correctly. Severity is low (workaround = use `=`),
  but it is a recurring agent-facing footgun and a CLI-consistency defect.
- Fix should address the read-CLI option parser class (session-logs `--limit`,
  list `--status`, and peers), not just session-logs, so the space form is
  accepted uniformly. Decide deliberately whether to standardize on accept-both
  (recommended, matches docs) or to reject space-form everywhere and fix the docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar session-logs --limit 30` (space form) returns the same result as
      `rebar session-logs --limit=30` (no "unknown option" error).
- [ ] Option-value parsing is consistent across the read-CLI commands in
      `reads_cli.py` (at minimum `session-logs --limit` and `list --status`
      accept the same form(s) as `ready --epic`).
- [ ] The chosen convention (accept-both vs equals-only) matches the documented
      usage in CLAUDE.md / `--help`; whichever is picked, docs and parser agree.
- [ ] A regression test covers both `--limit 30` and `--limit=30` for
      `session-logs` (and the analogous `list --status` space/equals forms).
compiled_state.title: CLI: 'session-logs --limit <n>' (space form) rejected as unknown option — read-command flags accept only --opt=value, inconsistent with write commands/docs
